summary: Add Store.ReplaceSingleton and use it for the single-envelope resources so each holds exactly one canonical row.
reason: An earlier sync stored recommendations/results-report under id=<resource-name> via the generic id-extraction fallback; the new code stored id='current', leaving TWO rows per type. loadAllResults concatenates every results-report row, so draws were silently DOUBLE-COUNTED across every transcend command (bundle/trend/goat/oscillating/pdf). ReplaceSingleton atomically purges prior rows + FTS entries before writing the canonical row.
validated_outcome: Live: exactly 1 row per type after --full; ApoB trend shows 2 draws (was 4); PDF 128 biomarkers / 3 rounds.
